Technique  ·  Ear Training  ·  Fretboard Knowledge

The Problem

Your Picking Falls Apart At Speed.

Most guitarists practice pentatonic runs the same way every time. Up and down. Same direction. Same tempo. It feels productive — but the moment you try to use those runs in a real musical context, the accuracy collapses.


The Looping Method at the heart of this book trains your picking in every direction, at every position, with built-in ear training so what you play actually sounds musical. No guesswork. Just a daily system that works.

54 pages of structured, notation-and-tab exercises built around a proven daily practice routine. Here's what's included:

FAQ

Common Questions.

How much time do I need each day?
The full daily workout is 45-60 minutes. If you have less time, the routine is structured so you can work through it in shorter blocks — even 20-30 minutes per session will produce results over time.
Do I need any prior experience?
You should be comfortable reading basic guitar tab and have some familiarity with alternate picking. Beginners will find it challenging — this is designed for players who already have some foundation and want to take their picking to the next level.
What makes this different from other books?
This is applied theory — not passive watching or generic exercises. The Looping Method trains your picking in real musical contexts, combining technique, ear training and fretboard knowledge in one daily system. Everything comes from my own experience developing these skills over 25+ years.
How do I get access?
After purchase you get instant access to the PDF download. The book is also available through Teachable. Guitar Pro files are available on request — just email me at jon@jonbjorkmusic.com.
Can I print it?
Yes — printing for personal use is allowed and encouraged. Many players find it easier to work from a printed copy at the practice desk.
What is your refund policy?
Because this is a digital download, I don't offer refunds. If you have any issues or questions after purchase, email me directly at jon@jonbjorkmusic.com and I'll help you out.
